assets.name: lecture_08_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 is the clearest and most useful frame. Susskind has moved far enough out of the way that the Hamiltonian expression and the adjacent curved sketch are both more visible, while his pointing hand still preserves the rhetorical emphasis on the kinetic term. It captures both the formula and the board layout with less occlusion than candidates 01 and 02, and with a slightly fuller left-side sketch than candidate 04.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: derivative of the Hamiltonian with respect to momentum

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 shows the most complete and useful board state in this subtitle window. The eigenvalue equations on the right board are the clearest and least truncated, and the left-side state-and-energy labels are still visible enough to preserve the lecture’s layout. Although Susskind’s paper partly covers the lower expression, this frame captures more finished mathematical content than the earlier candidates, which are either emptier or more transitional.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: That equals the Hamiltonian acting on psi.

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 02 is the clearest and most useful frame because the equation is fully visible, centered, and unobstructed. The other candidates include Susskind’s hand or the start of a new board mark, while this one isolates the written formula cleanly for note reconstruction.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: we also need the corresponding equation for the bra vector.

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 gives the best balance of legibility and completeness. The left-hand matrix-element expression with the time derivative term is clearer than in candidates 01 and 02, where Susskind’s arm obscures more of the right-hand side, and it preserves more of the surrounding board context than candidate 04. It is the most useful frame for reconstructing the lecture’s intermediate algebra about time derivatives and commutators with the Hamiltonian.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: time derivatives and commutators with Hamiltonians.
